reasons: Karrie is a strong answer because Barats relies heavily on HP and durability.
Repeated max-HP true damage punishes Barats when he walks forward to start fights.

proof.summary: Barats wants to become a durable frontliner through Big Guy stacks, while Karrie's Lightwheel Mark punishes high-HP targets with max-HP true damage.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel against Detona's Welcome.
Barats is forced to walk through Karrie's attack range.
failureCases: Barats's team zones Karrie away from the fight.

reasons: Lunox can damage durable targets through penetration-based magic pressure.
Barats struggles when the enemy mage can threaten him without needing to overcommit into his swallow range.

proof.summary: Barats wants to absorb damage and control space, while Lunox can use penetration-oriented magic damage to cut through his frontline durability.
worksBestWhen: Lunox positions outside Detona's Welcome range.
failureCases: Lunox is forced to spend defensive tools early.
Barats's team dives together and denies Lunox free casting.

reasons: Dyrroth can reduce Barats's physical defense and punish him before stacks become overwhelming.
This is especially relevant in lane or early skirmishes.

proof.summary: Barats wants to become harder to kill with stacks, while Dyrroth can reduce physical defense and create a burst window before Barats controls the fight.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth fights before Barats reaches strong stack uptime.
Dyrroth avoids being swallowed near enemy follow-up.
failureCases: Barats survives the burst and turns the fight long.
Dyrroth gets caught by Detona's Welcome.
Barats has full team support and stack uptime.

reasons: X.Borg can wear down Barats and threaten true damage through Last Insanity.
His armor gives him a buffer against Barats's close-range control if spaced correctly.

proof.summary: Barats wants to stand forward and absorb pressure, while X.Borg can kite with fire damage and threaten true-damage detonation.
worksBestWhen: X.Borg keeps Barats at the edge of his damage range.
Barats has already committed movement or ultimate.
failureCases: Barats swallows X.Borg before ultimate value.
Barats's team collapses on armorless X.Borg.

reasons: Valir can slow and push Barats away, reducing his ability to reach swallow range.
Barats is dangerous when he controls close space; Valir makes that space harder to enter.

proof.summary: Barats wants to walk into control range, while Valir can knock him back and force him to spend more time reaching targets.
worksBestWhen: Valir saves knockback for Barats's approach.
The team respects Detona's Welcome range.
failureCases: Barats flanks or catches Valir from fog.
Valir wastes Searing Torrent too early.
Barats's team layers engage after Valir's disengage.
